crisis.title: In immediate danger or thinking about suicide?
crisis.body: Call or text 988 now. Interpretation is available by request. If there is immediate physical danger, call 911.
crisis.bodyCompact: Call or text 988 now. Interpretation is available by request.

footer.privacyBody: Cầu Nối does not ask for names, emails, school IDs, addresses, immigration status, or medical record numbers. Tools are designed for anonymous guidance.

home.title: A multilingual mental-health navigator for CA-45 youth.
home.subtitle: Support for youth, families, and trusted adults across languages.
home.intro: Find support, practice family conversations, challenge stigma, and see community needs without creating an account or sharing identifying information.
home.pathIntro: Start with what feels closest to your situation. You can move around freely.
home.privacy: No login, no tracking forms, no names, no emails. Cầu Nối is a navigator, not a medical diagnosis tool.
home.bilingualBody: Youth may describe stress, sadness, or anxiety differently at school than at home. Immigrant parents may care deeply but have fewer familiar words for therapy, confidentiality, or crisis support. Multilingual resources help families slow down, understand each other, and find help without shame.
home.ca45Intro: CA-45 includes many immigrant families, students, caregivers, and community organizations working to support youth wellbeing. Cầu Nối focuses on community needs, youth voice, and access to care.
ca45Cards: Immigrant families may face cost concerns, transportation limits, unfamiliar systems, privacy worries, or uncertainty about which services are safe and appropriate for youth.
A teen may know how to describe stress in one language at school, while a caregiver may be more comfortable in another language. Stigma can make both generations avoid the conversation.
Helpful support respects language, family roles, faith, privacy, and lived experience while still giving youth a clear path to professional care when needed.
Anonymous survey responses help identify access gaps without asking for names, school names, contact details, or immigration status.
home.focusBody: The app centers youth who may feel caught between school stress, family expectations, language access, stigma, and uncertainty about where to begin. Every feature gives a concrete next step while respecting privacy and cultural context.
pathways: Find private, immediate, and school-based next steps without sharing your identity.
I am worried about a friend
Use simple scripts, warning signs, and crisis options to support someone safely.
I need to talk with family
Practice culturally respectful phrases before a hard conversation.
features: Built for immigrant families and all CA-45 youth.
Scripts help teens and caregivers talk before a crisis grows.
Core tools use practical, family-friendly language.
No accounts, no names, no emails, and no identifying form fields.
Each page points toward a small, realistic next step.

resourcesPage.title: Find CA-45 and Orange County support without giving your identity.
resourcesPage.subtitle: Local options organized by language, cost, resource type, city, and access format.
resourcesPage.body: Filter local options by language, cost, resource type, city, and in-person or virtual access. This page does not collect names, emails, addresses, or school IDs.
resourcesPage.intro: Resource information changes. Users should verify hours, eligibility, services, costs, and language access directly with providers before seeking care.

resourceFinder.searchPlaceholder: Search by name, city, service, or language
resourceFinder.privateNote: No search terms or personal details are saved. Filters only change what appears on this screen.
resourceFinder.verifyNote: Verify hours, eligibility, cost, and language access directly with providers before seeking care.
resourceFinder.title: CA-45 and Orange County resource finder
resourceFinder.emptyBody: Try resetting filters or selecting a broader resource type.

map.title: Map youth mental-health support across CA-45.
map.subtitle: High-tech filtering for language, insurance, age, city, and service type.
map.body: Explore counseling centers, school resources, crisis lines, community clinics, and bilingual services in a polished map view. Locations are approximate for planning.
map.fallback: Mapbox token is missing. Add NEXT_PUBLIC_MAPBOX_TOKEN in Vercel to load Explore and Satellite map styles.
map.note: Pins are approximate resource locations for planning. Verify details directly with providers.
map.noMatch: No map resources match those filters.

conversation.title: Practice a hard conversation before you have it.
conversation.subtitle: Side-by-side English and Vietnamese scripts for teens and families.
conversation.body: Choose a scenario and practice respectful language that reduces blame, lowers stigma, and helps everyone focus on support.

quiz.title: Reflect on mental-health stigma without sharing your identity.
quiz.subtitle: Eight quick questions, no names, no emails, no school names.
quiz.body: Your anonymous answers help show where youth may need clearer language, family conversation tools, or resource awareness.

dashboard.title: Anonymous insights can reveal access gaps.
dashboard.subtitle: Live, aggregate trends from anonymous survey responses.
dashboard.body: The dashboard shows only community-level patterns. It does not display names, emails, phone numbers, school names, IP addresses, or individual identities.

about.title: Built to connect youth, families, and support without collecting identity.
about.subtitle: A Congressional App Challenge project focused on access, language, and stigma reduction.
about.body: Cầu Nối is a youth mental-health navigator for CA-45 and Orange County. It helps users find resources, practice conversations, reflect on stigma, and understand community needs.
